elements.title: Reine Audu
elements.contents: ... that the fruit seller Reine Audu (pictured) was jailed after the march on Versailles in 1789 for announcing her intention to "[bring] back the Queen's head on her sword", among other charges?

elements.title: an abandoned château in an advanced state of ruin
elements.contents: ... that an abandoned château in an advanced state of ruin was saved with €500,000 from a lottery?

elements.title: stole a painting
elements.contents: ... that in 1976, thieves tied up an art collector, stole a painting, and were never caught?

elements.title: Petite-Émigrée
elements.contents: ... that mothers believed that taking young children to the grave of the Petite-Émigrée would help them to walk early?

elements.title: a French company
elements.contents: ... that a French company revived a favorite perfume of the ancient Roman nobility in 2023?

elements.title: a two-metre-high (6 ft 5 in) wall of dry stone
elements.contents: ... that a two-metre-high (6 ft 5 in) wall of dry stone was built in 1721 to stop the advance of the Great Plague of Marseille?

elements.title: Pierre Noël Lecauchois
elements.contents: ... that Pierre Noël Lecauchois's client was almost burned at the stake because the order to stay her execution was delayed?

elements.title: chicken in a pot
elements.contents: ... that Good King Henry allegedly said that he wanted every farmer to have a chicken in a pot?

elements.title: Pierre-Amable de Soubrany
elements.contents: ... that the corpse of Pierre-Amable de Soubrany was guillotined?
